name: cpio
title: cpio
summary: GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files
description:
GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files
GNU cpio is a tool for creating and extracting archives, or copying
files from one place to another.  It handles a number of cpio formats
as well as reading and writing tar files.

commands.name: cpio
commands.description: copy files to and from archives

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cpio because GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cpio as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cpio  {-o|--create} [-0acvABLV] [-C BYTES] [-H FORMAT] [-M MESSAGE] [-O [[USER@]HOST:]ARCHIVE]` as documented upstream for cpio.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cpio  {-i|--extract}  [-bcdfmnrtsuvBSV]  [-C  BYTES]  [-E  FILE]  [-H FORMAT] [-M MESSAGE] [-R` as documented upstream for cpio.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cpio  {-p|--pass-through}  [-0adlmuvLV]  [-R [USER][:.][GROUP]] [--null] [--reset-access-time]` as documented upstream for cpio.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cpio {-?|--help|--usage|--version}` as documented upstream for cpio.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cpio with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cpio --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: cpio - copy files to and from archives
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install cpio. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cpio  {-o|--create} [-0acvABLV] [-C BYTES] [-H FORMAT] [-M MESSAGE] [-O [[USER@]HOST:]ARCHIVE]`. Further invocations from upstream: `cpio  {-i|--extract}  [-bcdfmnrtsuvBSV]  [-C  BYTES]  [-E  FILE]  [-H FORMAT] [-M MESSAGE] [-R`; `cpio  {-p|--pass-through}  [-0adlmuvLV]  [-R [USER][:.][GROUP]] [--null] [--reset-access-time]`. GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: GNU cpio -- a program to manage archives of files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install cpio
Install with: sudo apt install cpio
